assertions.description: container-type: anchored is a recognised value.
anchored can be combined with size container-types such as inline-size.
@container anchored(fallback: none) parses as a valid conditional rule.
assertions.test: CSS.supports('selector(:not(*))') && (()=>{try{const s=new CSSStyleSheet();s.replaceSync('@container anchored(fallback: none) { .x { color: red; } }');return s.cssRules.length===1;}catch(_){return false;}})()

assertions.description: @container anchored(fallback: <dashed-ident>) parses, naming a position-try fallback.
assertions.test: (()=>{try{const s=new CSSStyleSheet();s.replaceSync('@container anchored(fallback: --flipped) { .x { color: red; } }');return s.cssRules.length===1;}catch(_){return false;}})()

assertions.description: @container anchored(fallback: flip-block) parses, naming a try-tactic fallback.
assertions.test: (()=>{try{const s=new CSSStyleSheet();s.replaceSync('@container anchored(fallback: flip-block) { .x { color: red; } }');return s.cssRules.length===1;}catch(_){return false;}})()

assertions.description: @container anchored(not (fallback: none)) parses as a boolean-combined query.
assertions.test: (()=>{try{const s=new CSSStyleSheet();s.replaceSync('@container anchored(not (fallback: none)) { .x { color: red; } }');return s.cssRules.length===1;}catch(_){return false;}})()
